definition: Not damaged or impaired in any way; complete.
example: Despite the rough journey, the antique vase arrived intact.

definition: Though; although; even if.
example: He finally accepted the job, albeit with some hesitation.

definition: Very complicated or detailed.
example: The watchmaker specialized in intricate designs that required immense precision.

definition: The action or fact of achieving a goal toward which one has worked.
example: The attainment of his PhD was the culmination of many years of hard work.

definition: To restrain or prevent someone or something from doing something.
example: She tried to hold back her tears during the emotional farewell.

definition: Continuing for an extended period or without interruption.
example: The company reported sustained growth over the last three quarters.

definition: To understand (a difficult idea or concept) fully and completely.
example: It took him a while to grasp the complex instructions.

definition: In a clearly visible way; attracting notice or attention.
example: The new skyscraper stood conspicuously tall against the city skyline.

definition: To cause (an event or situation, typically one that is bad or undesirable) to happen suddenly, unexpectedly, or prematurely.
example: The economic downturn precipitated a wave of bankruptcies.

definition: To engage or be connected with one another by overlapping or by fitting together.
example: The gears of the machine interlock perfectly to ensure smooth operation.

definition: Relevant or applicable to a particular matter; apposite.
example: Please stick to the pertinent details and avoid tangents.

definition: Relevant to a subject under consideration; closely related.
example: Her comments were germane to the discussion and helped us reach a conclusion.

definition: To solidify or thicken, especially by cooling or freezing.
example: The gravy began to congeal as it cooled on the plate.

definition: To make or become stiff or rigid.
example: The cold air made his fingers stiffen, making it hard to tie his shoelaces.

definition: To express (a speech or text) in fewer words; to make more dense or concentrated.
example: He had to condense the long report into a concise summary.

definition: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems.
example: They were wary of strangers approaching their isolated cabin.

definition: Acting with or showing care and thought for the future.
example: It's prudent to save money for retirement rather than spending it all immediately.

definition: Careful and circumspect in one's speech or actions, especially in order to avoid causing offense or to gain an advantage.
example: He made a few discreet inquiries about the job opening before applying.

definition: A kingdom; a field or domain of activity or interest.
example: The issue falls within the realm of international law.

definition: Excessive and insincere praise, especially that given to further one's own interests.
example: She saw through his flattery and knew he only wanted a favor.

definition: To use up the supply or resources of.
example: Logging has severely depleted the forest's natural resources.

definition: In large quantities or amounts.
example: She took notes copiously during the professor's lecture.

definition: In an energetic, lively, and cheerful manner.
example: The children played exuberantly in the park after the rain.

definition: The outer limits or edge of an area or object.
example: The city's growth pushed its boundaries to the periphery of the old town.

definition: The outer, marginal, or extreme part of an area, group, or subject.
example: He lives on the fringe of society, rarely interacting with others.

definition: Apparently or seemingly, but perhaps not actually.
example: Ostensibly, the meeting was about new strategies, but it quickly devolved into a blame game.

definition: On the surface; externally; apparently.
example: Outwardly, he seemed calm, but inwardly he was very anxious.

definition: (Especially of a deity or spirit) embodied in flesh; in human form.
example: Many believed the charismatic leader was evil incarnate.

definition: To escape or avoid (someone or something) by cleverness or trickery.
example: He managed to evade capture by hiding in the dense jungle.

definition: To avoid (someone or something) with a sudden quick movement; to avoid by evasion.
example: He had to dodge a car that suddenly swerved into his lane.

definition: To persistently avoid, ignore, or reject (someone or something) through antipathy or caution.
example: After the scandal, many of his former friends began to shun him.

definition: To make (someone) legally or morally bound to an action or course of action.
example: The law obliges parents to send their children to school.

definition: In a private and personal way; in a way that shows a close understanding or familiarity.
example: He intimately knew the workings of the old machine.

definition: Hesitating or doubting; not to be relied upon; suspect.
example: The claims made in the advertisement seemed rather dubious.

definition: To discover or identify the presence or existence of.
example: The smoke detector was able to detect the fire early on.

definition: To jump or dive quickly and energetically; to fall or drop straight down at high speed.
example: She watched the diver plunge into the cold water.

definition: To dip or submerge in a liquid; to involve oneself deeply in a particular activity or interest.
example: He likes to immerse himself in a good book for hours.

definition: The establishment or starting point of an institution or activity.
example: The project has been successful since its inception.

definition: Causing or capable of causing death.
example: The venom of this snake is lethal if not treated immediately.

definition: Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way.
example: The pernicious influence of social media on young minds is a growing concern.

definition: Being a part of a whole; an essential part or element.
example: Oxygen and hydrogen are constituent elements of water.

definition: The action or fact of treating someone unfairly in order to benefit from their work or resources; the action of making use of and benefiting from resources.
example: The company was accused of the exploitation of its low-wage workers.

definition: An object surviving from an earlier time, especially one of historical or sentimental interest.
example: The old typewriter in the attic was a relic of a bygone era.

definition: A trace or remnant of something that is disappearing or no longer exists.
example: The ancient ruins were the last vestige of a forgotten civilization.

definition: A small remaining quantity of something.
example: There was only a small remnant of fabric left after she finished sewing the dress.

definition: To cause something to change direction after hitting it; to cause someone to deviate from an intended purpose.
example: The goalkeeper managed to deflect the ball away from the goal.

definition: To cause someone or something to change course or turn from one direction to another; to entertain or amuse.
example: The police had to divert traffic due to the accident on the main road.

definition: To apply or bring to bear (a force, influence, or quality).
example: He had to exert a lot of force to open the stubborn jar.

definition: To hold and use (a weapon or tool); to have and exercise (power or influence).
example: The knight expertly wielded his sword in battle.

definition: (Of a quality or state) existing but not yet developed or manifest; hidden or concealed.
example: He possessed a latent talent for music that he only discovered in his twenties.

definition: Occurring again periodically or repeatedly.
example: The recurring dream always left her feeling uneasy.

definition: Occurring often or repeatedly.
example: His recurrent headaches were a cause for concern, prompting him to see a doctor.

definition: (Of something provided or available) lacking in quantity or quality; sparse.
example: The refugees lived on a meager diet of rice and beans.

definition: Not having enough of a specified quality or ingredient; insufficient or inadequate.
example: The soil was deficient in key nutrients, leading to poor crop growth.

definition: To prove more powerful or superior; to be widespread in a particular area at a particular time.
example: Justice will ultimately prevail in this case.

definition: Widespread in a particular area or at a particular time; common.
example: The use of smartphones is highly prevalent among teenagers today.

definition: Individually separate and distinct.
example: The research involved several discrete steps, each requiring careful attention.

definition: A heavy object attached to a rope or chain and used to moor a vessel to the seabed; a person or thing that provides stability or security.
example: The family farm was his anchor, always pulling him back home.

definition: Not likely to be true or to happen.
example: It's highly improbable that they will finish the project on time.

definition: Not disputed or called into question; accepted or recognized by everyone.
example: He is the undisputed champion of the lightweight division.

definition: Not leading to a firm conclusion; not decisive.
example: The evidence presented in court was inconclusive, so the jury could not reach a verdict.

definition: An essential, indispensable, or fundamental building block or basis.
example: Education is the cornerstone of a successful society.

definition: A set of ideas, figures, or devices that provides support or a basis for something.
example: Strong moral values form the underpinning of their family life.

definition: Without intention; accidentally.
example: She inadvertently deleted the important file while cleaning up her computer.

definition: (Of a place) not seen or visited by many people; sheltered and private.
example: They rented a secluded cabin in the woods for their vacation.

definition: The invention of a new word or phrase.
example: The word 'podcast' is a relatively recent coinage.

definition: To restore friendly relations between; to make (one thing) compatible or consistent with (another).
example: It was difficult to reconcile his past actions with his current claims of innocence.

definition: Surpassing all others; very distinguished in some way.
example: She is recognized as the preeminent scholar in her field.

definition: To increase in size, number, strength, or intensity.
example: As the moon began to wax, its crescent grew larger each night.

definition: To be quick to do something; to move or travel quickly.
example: She hastened to finish her work so she could leave early.

definition: Subject to further confirmation; not definitely; in a hesitant manner.
example: He tentatively approached the stray dog, offering it a piece of food.

definition: The feeling or belief that someone or something is worthless or despicable; contempt.
example: She felt nothing but scorn for his deceptive tactics.

definition: The feeling that someone or something is unworthy of one's consideration or respect; contempt.
example: She looked at him with disdain, refusing to acknowledge his presence.

definition: A contemptuous or mocking smile, remark, or tone.
example: He gave a sneer as he walked past his rival, muttering a rude comment.

definition: To feel contempt or a deep repugnance for.
example: She despises dishonesty and cannot tolerate people who lie.

definition: A hidden or unsuspected danger or difficulty.
example: One of the main pitfalls of starting a new business is underestimating the competition.

definition: A completely empty space; a feeling of emptiness.
example: After his children left for college, he felt a void in his life.

definition: Large or important enough to be noticed or measured.
example: There has been an appreciable increase in the company's profits this quarter.

definition: To lead an attack or a movement or campaign.
example: The organization decided to spearhead a new initiative to promote environmental awareness.

definition: To severely reduce the strength or effectiveness of (something) by killing or destroying a large proportion of it.
example: The disease had decimated the rabbit population in the region.

definition: To get or bring (something) back from somewhere.
example: The dog was trained to retrieve pheasants shot by hunters.

definition: Able to be perceived or recognized; noticeable.
example: There was no discernible difference between the two brands of coffee.

definition: To demand or specify (a requirement), typically as part of an agreement.
example: The contract stipulates that employees must give three months' notice if they wish to resign.

definition: To display or show (a quality or feeling) by one's acts or appearance; demonstrate.
example: Her unhappiness was manifest in her withdrawn behavior.

definition: (Of a person or thing) completely developed or established; fully qualified.
example: After years of training, she became a full-fledged doctor.

definition: Fully developed; complete.
example: What started as a minor disagreement soon escalated into a full-blown argument.

definition: (Especially of a task, process, or journey) requiring considerable effort and time.
example: Moving all the furniture was a long and laborious task.

definition: Extremely impressive or attractive.
example: The view from the top of the mountain was absolutely stunning.

definition: So impressive, beautiful, or exciting as to take one's breath away.
example: The panoramic views of the valley were truly breathtaking.

definition: Possessing a quality that arouses or stimulates desire or interest but remains unobtainable or beyond one's reach.
example: The tantalizing aroma of freshly baked bread filled the kitchen.

definition: Attractive or tempting; alluring.
example: The enticing aroma of cinnamon rolls made it hard to resist.

definition: Playfully provoking or making fun of someone.
example: My younger brother was always teasing me about my messy hair.

definition: In an open and honest manner; frankly.
example: She spoke candidly about her struggles with depression.

definition: In a manner that is required by law or a rule; obligatorily.
example: All children must compulsorily attend school until a certain age.

definition: Utterly absurd or ridiculous.
example: The idea that he could be involved in the robbery was preposterous.

definition: Thinly dispersed or scattered.
example: The vegetation on the desert island was sparse.

definition: Small or insufficient in quantity or amount.
example: The evidence against him was rather scanty.

definition: (Of something provided or available) lacking in quantity or quality.
example: He earns a meager salary that barely covers his rent.

definition: More important than anything else in a particular situation.
example: The safety of the children was the overriding concern.

definition: To have a tendency to favor or prefer something.
example: She tends to lean toward more traditional styles in her artwork.

definition: Matter that settles to the bottom of a liquid; dregs.
example: There was a thick layer of sediment at the bottom of the old wine bottle.

definition: To be the strongest or main element; to be greater in number or amount.
example: In the tropical forest, green colors predominate.

definition: (Of a route or journey) longer than the most direct way.
example: The taxi driver took a circuitous route to avoid the rush hour traffic.

definition: An individual animal, plant, or mineral used as an example of its species or type for scientific study or display.
example: The botanist collected a rare specimen of a tropical flower.

definition: Following one after another in uninterrupted succession; successive.
example: She won the championship for the third consecutive year.

definition: Having no name; unnamed.
example: The old map marked several innominate rivers that explorers had yet to name.

definition: The amount of something allocated to a particular person or purpose.
example: Each student received an allotment of three books from the library.

definition: (Of a liquid) flow or leak slowly through porous material or small holes.
example: Water began to seep through the cracks in the dam.

definition: (Of a fluid) slowly trickle or seep out of something; to flow slowly and steadily.
example: Blood started to ooze from the wound.

definition: Full of energy and enthusiasm; bright and striking.
example: The city has a vibrant nightlife and a diverse art scene.

definition: (Especially of a woman) attractively lively and animated.
example: Her vivacious personality made her the life of every party.

definition: A supreme ruler, especially a monarch; possessing ultimate power.
example: The sovereign power of the state rests with its citizens.

definition: Confirm or give support to (a statement, theory, or finding).
example: Witness accounts corroborated the police's theory about the accident.

definition: Provide evidence to support or prove the truth of.
example: The report failed to substantiate the claims of fraud.

definition: Begin or introduce (a system, policy, or period); admit (someone) formally to public office.
example: The new president will be inaugurated next month.

definition: Bring to completion or reality; satisfy (a hope, desire, or promise).
example: She worked hard to fulfill her dream of becoming a doctor.

definition: Put (a decision, plan, agreement, etc.) into effect.
example: The company decided to implement a new marketing strategy.

definition: Speak with excessive pride and self-satisfaction about one's achievements, possessions, or abilities.
example: He liked to boast about his extensive collection of vintage cars.

definition: Say something in a way that shows excessive pride in oneself or one's achievements. (Often informal and interchangeable with 'boast').
example: She couldn't help but brag about her perfect test score.

definition: Imagine as a future possibility; visualize.
example: He envisioned a world where everyone had access to clean water.

definition: Form or devise (a plan or idea) in the mind; form a concept of.
example: She conceived the idea for her novel while on vacation.

definition: Beginning to grow or increase rapidly; flourishing.
example: The burgeoning tech industry is creating many new jobs.

definition: Not having any serious purpose or value; carefree and superficial.
example: He made a frivolous purchase of an expensive gadget he didn't need.

definition: All future generations of people.
example: We must preserve these artifacts for posterity.

definition: Make (a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling) worse.
example: Loud noise can exacerbate the stress of city life.

definition: Situated far from a center or main point; remote.
example: The company has several factories in outlying areas.

definition: In a way that shows great attention to detail; very thoroughly.
example: The artist meticulously painted every tiny detail of the landscape.

definition: Be (a part) of a whole; form or make up.
example: Women constitute a majority of the student body at this university.

definition: Given, felt, or done in return; mutual.
example: Their relationship was built on reciprocal trust and respect.

definition: The state or tendency of being likely to do or suffer from something.
example: His proneness to accidents made his family worry.

definition: Make an action or process happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly.
example: We need to expedite the delivery of these essential supplies.

definition: The action of driving or pushing forward.
example: The boat uses jet propulsion to move through the water.

definition: Push (something or someone) suddenly or violently in a specified direction.
example: The rocket generated immense thrust to escape Earth's gravity.

definition: The force or energy with which a body moves; a driving force or stimulus.
example: The discovery of new resources gave fresh impetus to the economy.

definition: Admirably purposeful, determined, and unwavering.
example: She remained resolute in her decision to pursue her dream despite the obstacles.

definition: Steady or resolute; not wavering.
example: His unwavering commitment to justice inspired many others.

definition: Seeming reasonable or probable.
example: Her excuse for being late sounded plausible, so I believed her.

definition: To add to something in a way that improves or completes it.
example: The new curtains perfectly complement the existing decor of the living room.

definition: Very noticeable or marked; strong.
example: There was a pronounced change in her attitude after she received the promotion.

definition: Subject to chance; uncertain or risky.
example: Investing in that startup is a chancy venture, but it could pay off big.

definition: To continue firmly or obstinately in an opinion or a course of action in spite of difficulty or opposition.
example: If you persist with your studies, you will eventually master the language.

definition: To perceive or recognize something.
example: It was hard to discern the truth from all the conflicting reports.

definition: Having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations or people and turn this to one's advantage.
example: The astute businesswoman quickly recognized the market opportunity.

definition: Having or showing good judgment; perceptive.
example: A discerning art critic can distinguish between genuine masterpieces and mere imitations.

definition: Having or showing sensitive insight; quick to understand.
example: Her perceptive comments revealed a deep understanding of human nature.

definition: Serving as a substitute or replacement.
example: The company offered a substitutive product when the original was out of stock.

definition: So delicate or precise as to be difficult to analyze or describe; (of a distinction or a difference) fine or delicate.
example: There was a subtle difference in flavor between the two wines.

definition: Making a certain situation or outcome likely or possible.
example: A quiet environment is conducive to studying.

definition: Deeply involved in; absorbed in.
example: She was so immersed in her book that she didn't hear the doorbell.

definition: Thinking or worrying about something a lot, to the exclusion of other things.
example: He was so preoccupied with his work that he forgot his wife's birthday.

definition: (of a person or animal) Moving or capable of moving with great speed.
example: The gazelle is a fleet animal, capable of outrunning most predators.

definition: A person or thing that is likely to cause harm; a threat or danger.
example: The growing instability in the region is a menace to global peace.

definition: To frighten or overawe (someone), especially in order to make them do what one wants.
example: The bully tried to intimidate the smaller child into giving him his lunch money.

definition: Similar or related to.
example: Her feeling of relief was akin to winning the lottery after a long period of bad luck.

definition: To praise formally or officially.
example: The principal wished to commend the students for their outstanding performance.

definition: The process or state of breaking or being broken into small or separate parts.
example: The fragmentation of the political party led to its eventual decline.

definition: To spread and promote (an idea, theory, etc.) widely.
example: The organization works to propagate its message of environmental awareness.

definition: To prove (a statement or theory) to be wrong or false; disprove.
example: She was able to refute the accusations with solid evidence.

definition: To claim or prove that (evidence or an accusation) is false.
example: The lawyer tried to rebut the prosecutor's arguments with new testimony.

word: on the spur of the moment
definition: Without planning or prior thought; impulsively.
example: We decided to go on a road trip on the spur of the moment.

definition: Because of a sudden strong desire without thinking about the consequences.
example: He bought the expensive watch on impulse, only to regret it later.

definition: (of weather) mild; (of a person or act) merciful.
example: The clement weather made for a pleasant day at the beach.

definition: Relating to or denoting a region or climate characterized by mild temperatures.
example: Japan has a temperate climate, with distinct seasons.

definition: In agreement or harmony with.
example: His actions were not congruent with his stated beliefs.

definition: To continue in a course of action despite difficulty or delay in achieving success.
example: Despite facing many setbacks, she continued to persevere with her dream.

definition: (of an unwelcome development) to be in the process of forming or developing.
example: Trouble was brewing between the two rival factions.

definition: (of an event regarded as ominous or threatening) to seem about to happen.
example: A storm began to loom on the horizon.

definition: Preconceived opinion that is not based on reason or actual experience.
example: It's important to overcome prejudice and treat everyone fairly.

definition: To encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behavior).
example: The speaker was accused of trying to incite a riot.

definition: To stimulate or give rise to (a reaction or emotion, typically a strong or unwelcome one) in someone.
example: His rude comments were intended to provoke an argument.

definition: To absorb all the attention or interest of.
example: The captivating novel completely engrossed her for hours.

definition: To attribute something to; to regard as belonging to or originating from.
example: He ascribed his success to hard work and a bit of luck.

definition: To represent (something, especially an undesirable quality or action) as being done or possessed by someone; attribute.
example: The police imputed the crime to the suspect despite a lack of direct evidence.

definition: To emit wind from the stomach through the mouth; to erupt forcefully.
example: After finishing the large meal, he let out a loud belch.

definition: To express (an emotion), especially anger, in a forceful and often unrestrained way.
example: She called her friend to vent her frustration about the difficult day.

definition: To release or allow to flow; to dismiss someone from a job or service.
example: The factory was fined for discharging pollutants into the river.

definition: Extremely large or great, especially in scale or degree.
example: The immense size of the universe is difficult to comprehend.

definition: Full of activity; moving in an energetic and busy manner.
example: The market was bustling with shoppers and vendors early in the morning.

definition: Individually separate and distinct.
example: The project is divided into several discrete phases, each with its own goals.

definition: Large or heavy and therefore difficult to carry or use; slow or complicated and therefore inefficient.
example: The old computer system was cumbersome and often crashed.

definition: A thing that is helpful or beneficial; a blessing.
example: The new public library will be a great boon to the community.

definition: To release from a leash or restraint; to release or let loose a powerful force.
example: The general decided to unleash his full army on the enemy.

definition: A person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand.
example: His sudden disappearance remains an enigma to his family and friends.

definition: Resolutely or dutifully firm and unwavering.
example: She remained steadfast in her commitment to the cause, despite the challenges.

definition: Not wavering; steady or resolute.
example: Her unwavering support gave him the confidence to pursue his dreams.

definition: Admirably purposeful, determined, and unwavering.
example: He remained resolute in his decision to leave the company.

definition: Delicately small and pretty; elegant and refined.
example: The child held the dainty teacup with great care.

definition: Extremely beautiful and delicate; intensely felt.
example: The artist created an exquisite painting with intricate details.

definition: Something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected.
example: The warm weather in December was an anomaly for the region.

definition: A close and harmonious relationship in which the people or groups concerned understand each other's feelings or ideas and communicate well.
example: The teacher established a good rapport with her students, making them feel comfortable.

definition: To provide or serve as clear evidence of; to declare that something exists or is true.
example: The old documents attest to the historical significance of the building.

definition: To strengthen (a place) with defensive works so as to protect it against attack; to strengthen or invigorate (someone or something).
example: The villagers decided to fortify their homes against the approaching storm.

definition: To a notable degree; very.
example: She is an eminently qualified candidate for the position due to her extensive experience.

definition: The process or skill of taking one number or amount away from another.
example: The math problem required him to perform several subtractions.

definition: The action of deducting or subtracting something; the process of reaching a conclusion by reasoning.
example: After careful deduction, the detective identified the culprit.

definition: A practical and approximate principle or method based on experience rather than exact measurement.
example: As a rule of thumb, you should save at least three months' worth of living expenses.

definition: To receive and pass on (information or a message).
example: The dispatcher relayed the urgent message to the police officers.

definition: To communicate (information); to make known; to bestow (a quality).
example: The wise old man sought to impart his knowledge to the younger generation.

definition: Having or showing a strict regard for what is right; principled; diligently attentive to details.
example: She was scrupulous in her work, checking every detail for accuracy.

definition: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; very concerned about cleanliness.
example: He was a fastidious dresser, always ensuring his clothes were perfectly pressed.

definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
example: The scientist conducted the experiment with meticulous care, recording every tiny observation.

definition: To split or sever (something) along a natural line or grain; to adhere firmly and closely or loyally and unwaveringly.
example: The axe was used to cleave the log in half; they cleaved to their traditions despite outside pressures.

definition: To drive or force (an attack or attacker) back or away; to disgust or be distasteful to.
example: The strong fortress walls helped to repel the invading army.

definition: To successfully defend oneself against an attack or repel an unwanted advance.
example: The boxer managed to beat off his opponent's relentless attacks.

definition: Farthest from the center or inside.
example: The outermost layer of the Earth's atmosphere is called the exosphere.

definition: Able to be seen or noticed, especially slightly or subtly.
example: There was a perceptible change in his mood after he received the good news.

definition: A thing that persuades or influences someone to do something.
example: The company offered a significant financial inducement to attract top talent.

definition: Something that tempts or entices a person or animal to do something or to go somewhere, especially something that is bad or dangerous.
example: The prospect of a high salary was a powerful lure for the young graduates.

definition: (Of a loss, injury, or damage) impossible to repair or put right.
example: The fire caused irreparable damage to the historic building.

definition: Impossible to cure or put right.
example: His illness was diagnosed as irremediable, leaving little hope for recovery.

definition: The highest or climatic point of something, especially as attained after a long time.
example: The award ceremony was the culmination of years of hard work and dedication.

definition: The top or highest part of something, especially a mountain or a pyramid.
example: The climbers reached the apex of the mountain just as the sun began to set.

definition: Loyalty or commitment of a subordinate to a superior or of an individual to a group or cause.
example: Soldiers swear an oath of allegiance to their country.

definition: A mode of behavior or way of thought peculiar to an individual.
example: One of his idiosyncrasies was always wearing mismatched socks.

definition: The quality of being unconventional and slightly strange.
example: Her artistic eccentricity made her stand out in the conservative community.

definition: A strange or peculiar person, thing, or quality.
example: The old house was full of interesting oddities collected over many years.

definition: Tending to cause harm; harmful.
example: Smoking is highly detrimental to your health.

definition: Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way.
example: The pernicious influence of social media on young minds is a growing concern.

definition: (Of a river or road) follow a winding course; (of a person) wander aimlessly or idly.
example: The stream meandered gently through the meadow.

definition: Cause someone to become hostile.
example: Try not to antagonize your boss if you want to keep your job.

definition: Make (a problem, injury, or offense) worse or more serious.
example: His constant complaining only served to aggravate the tense situation.

definition: Have an intense feeling of longing for something, typically something that one has lost or been separated from.
example: She yearned for the comfort of her childhood home.

definition: Suffer a mental and physical decline, especially from a broken heart; yearn for.
example: He still pines for his lost love years after their breakup.

definition: Formally declare one's abandonment of (a claim, right, or possession); refuse to continue to own, use, or have (something).
example: He decided to renounce his citizenship and move to another country.

definition: Voluntarily cease to keep or claim; give up.
example: She had to relinquish her ownership of the company.

definition: The action or fact of maintaining or supporting oneself at a minimum level.
example: Many families in the region rely on subsistence farming to survive.

definition: According to the customs or usual practices associated with a particular society, place, or set of circumstances.
example: It is customary to shake hands when meeting someone new in many cultures.

definition: Open to more than one interpretation; ambiguous; uncertain or ambiguous in nature.
example: His equivocal answer left us unsure of his true intentions.

definition: Allow (a liquid, gas, or other substance) to flow out from where it has been confined; release (someone) from a duty or institution.
example: The factory was fined for discharging pollutants into the river.

definition: (Of a person) display (an emotion or quality) strongly and openly; (of a substance) ooze out.
example: He exuded confidence as he walked onto the stage.

definition: Be responsible for the management, direction, or oversight of; supervise.
example: A team of engineers was appointed to superintend the construction of the new bridge.

definition: Having an uneven surface; full of bumps.
example: The car ride was bumpy due to the unpaved road.

definition: (Of a substance or fabric) rough or loose in texture or grain; (of a person or their speech) rude, crude, or vulgar.
example: The old blanket was made of coarse wool.

definition: (Of a landscape) having a broken, uneven, and rocky surface; (of a person) having a strong, sturdy physique.
example: The hikers faced a challenging journey through the rugged terrain.

definition: Prevent something from happening or make something impossible.
example: His severe allergy to nuts precludes him from eating many common desserts.

definition: Able to be assumed or supposed; probable.
example: The presumable reason for his absence was illness.

definition: In a way that is planned or contrived to achieve a particular effect.
example: She smiled calculatedly, knowing exactly what she was doing.

definition: A departure from what is normal, usual, or expected, typically an unwelcome one.
example: His sudden outburst was an aberration from his usual calm demeanor.

definition: The action of leaving; a deviation from an accepted, prescribed, or normal course of action.
example: This new policy marks a significant departure from previous strategies.

definition: A very thin line, crack, or gap; (of a fracture) very thin or fine.
example: The doctor identified a hairline fracture in his wrist.

definition: Create (a relationship or new conditions) or produce a fraudulent copy or imitation.
example: The two nations worked together to forge a strong alliance.

definition: Having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations or people and turn this to one's advantage.
example: An astute businessman, he quickly recognized the market opportunity.

definition: Having or showing sharp powers of judgment; astute.
example: She made a shrewd investment that yielded significant returns.

definition: Having or showing an accurate and deep understanding; perceptive.
example: Her insightful analysis helped us understand the complex problem.

definition: An indirect or passing reference.
example: The poem contains an allusion to Greek mythology.

definition: Movement or the ability to move from one place to another.
example: The invention of the wheel greatly improved human locomotion.

definition: An act of violent or open resistance to an established government or ruler.
example: The rebellion was quickly suppressed by the government forces.

definition: An act of rebellion; rise in rebellion.
example: The peasants decided to revolt against the oppressive regime.

definition: An act of resistance or rebellion; a revolt.
example: The student uprising spread across the capital city.

definition: The action of provoking unlawful behavior or urging someone to behave unlawfully.
example: The speaker was charged with incitement to riot after encouraging the crowd to march on the capitol.

definition: Deriving ideas, style, or taste from a broad and diverse range of sources.
example: Her music collection was truly eclectic, ranging from classical to heavy metal.

definition: Many and various; numerous and diverse.
example: The problems facing the community were manifold, requiring a multifaceted solution.

definition: Very complicated or detailed.
example: The clockmaker carefully assembled the intricate gears and springs of the antique watch.

definition: (Of a bad, difficult, or unwelcome situation or phenomenon) present or experienced to a severe or intense degree.
example: The hospital was facing an acute shortage of nurses during the pandemic.

definition: Resembling a palace in being spacious and splendid.
example: They lived in a palatial mansion with vast gardens and a private lake.

definition: To flow or move quickly through something.
example: A shiver of fear began to course through her veins as she heard the strange noise.

definition: Without being aware; unintentionally.
example: She unwittingly revealed a secret that her friend had asked her to keep confidential.

definition: To make full use of and derive benefit from (a resource); or to use a person or situation in an unfair or selfish way.
example: The company was accused of exploiting its workers by paying them very low wages.

definition: Angry or bitter disagreement; conflict.
example: The civil war brought years of internal strife and suffering to the nation.

definition: A violent confrontation or argument; or to come into conflict.
example: There was a loud clash of cymbals, signaling the start of the performance.

definition: To appear as a vague, menacing, or enlarged object, typically through a mist or in the dark; or to appear as a large, often threatening, presence.
example: A storm cloud began to loom on the horizon, threatening to ruin their picnic.

definition: In an honest and direct way, often even when the truth is uncomfortable.
example: He spoke candidly about his struggles with mental health, hoping to inspire others.

definition: To regard or consider in a specified way.
example: The court did not deem his actions to be criminal, but rather a regrettable mistake.

definition: To temporarily prevent from continuing or being in force or effect; or to hang (something) from somewhere.
example: The school decided to suspend the student for a week due to his repeated misbehavior.

definition: To hang or swing loosely.
example: The child let his legs dangle over the edge of the swing set as he talked.

definition: To prevent something from happening or make something impossible.
example: His poor attendance record will preclude him from receiving the award.

definition: To keep away from or stop oneself from doing (something).
example: She tried to avoid eye contact with him after their argument.

definition: To turn away (one's eyes or thoughts); or to prevent or ward off (an unwelcome occurrence).
example: He quickly averted his gaze when he saw his ex-girlfriend across the room.

definition: Using or containing too many words.
example: His essay was criticized for being wordy and lacking conciseness.

definition: Unnecessary, especially through being more than enough.
example: Deleting superfluous words can make your writing much stronger and clearer.

definition: Exuberantly plentiful; abundant.
example: She offered profuse apologies for her late arrival to the meeting.

definition: Extremely rich, elaborate, or luxurious; or to give something in generous or extravagant quantities.
example: They threw a lavish party to celebrate their 50th wedding anniversary.

definition: To severely damage or destroy (a vehicle, building, or other structure); or the destruction of a ship or vehicle at sea.
example: The car was a complete wreck after colliding with the tree.

definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities of food; or having a very eager approach to an activity.
example: He was a voracious reader, finishing several books a week.

definition: (Of an appetite or desire) impossible to satisfy.
example: His insatiable curiosity led him to explore many different fields of study.

definition: Excessively greedy for food.
example: The gluttonous child ate three slices of cake and still wanted more.

definition: The act or process of making something better.
example: The charity is dedicated to the betterment of educational opportunities for underprivileged children.

definition: To make (something bad or unsatisfactory) better.
example: Steps were taken to ameliorate the poor working conditions in the factory.

definition: To make minor changes to (a text, piece of legislation, etc.) in order to make it fairer or more accurate, or to improve it.
example: The politician proposed to amend the law to include stronger environmental protections.

definition: A member of a constituency; or a component part of something.
example: Oxygen and hydrogen are constituent elements of water.

definition: Showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect.
example: The teacher punished the student for his insolent behavior during class.

definition: Not showing proper respect; rude.
example: His impertinent questions about her personal life made her uncomfortable.

definition: Not showing due respect for another person; impertinent.
example: The impudent child stuck out his tongue at the principal.

definition: To produce or be filled with a deep, full, reverberating sound; or to evoke or suggest images, memories, and emotions.
example: His powerful speech resonated with the audience, inspiring them to action.

definition: To say something again or a number of times, typically for emphasis or clarity.
example: The teacher had to reiterate the instructions several times before everyone understood.

definition: Poorly made or built; of poor quality.
example: The shoddy construction of the building led to its collapse during the earthquake.

definition: To drink (alcohol); or to absorb or assimilate (ideas or knowledge).
example: She loved to imbibe knowledge from various books and documentaries.

definition: Enthusiastic or passionate.
example: He was an ardent supporter of the local football team, attending every match.

definition: Having or displaying a passionate intensity.
example: She delivered a fervent prayer for peace and healing during the ceremony.

definition: No longer existing or functioning.
example: The old factory, now defunct, stood as a monument to a bygone industrial era.

definition: To scatter or spread things untidily over a surface or area.
example: She strewed papers all over her desk.

definition: To enclose something in or as if in a capsule; to express the essential features of something in a brief way.
example: The report managed to encapsulate the entire problem in a few paragraphs.

definition: To make something denser or more concentrated; to express in fewer words.
example: Please condense your report to two pages.

definition: Difficult to carry or manage because of its size, shape, or weight.
example: The box was unwieldy and hard to carry up the stairs.

definition: Lasting for a very short time.
example: The beauty of a sunset is ephemeral.

definition: The fact or condition of being severe; strictness, harshness, or intensity.
example: The severity of the punishment surprised everyone.

definition: Based on, concerned with, or verifiable by observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic.
example: There is no empirical evidence to support his claims.

definition: To subject someone to an unpleasant experience or test; to connect a telephone call.
example: The training program really put the new recruits through their paces.

definition: To discharge or emit (a substance or smell) slowly and steadily; to display (an emotion or quality) strongly and openly.
example: The flowers exuded a sweet fragrance.

definition: To come before something in time, order, or position.
example: The warm-up act will precede the main performance.

definition: Dried out with heat; extremely thirsty.
example: After the long hike, my throat was parched.

definition: (Of a bad thing or feeling) present or experienced to a severe or intense degree; having or showing a perceptive understanding or insight.
example: She felt an acute sense of loss after her pet died.

definition: To make amends to someone for loss or harm suffered; compensate.
example: The company offered to recompense customers for the faulty product.

definition: The action of paying back money that has been borrowed.
example: The loan requires a monthly repayment.

definition: To think deeply or focus one's mind for a period, especially for spiritual or religious purposes or as a method of relaxation.
example: She meditates for 30 minutes every morning.

definition: The first principles of a subject; the basic elements or skills.
example: He learned the rudiments of programming.

definition: Awkward in movement or handling things.
example: The clumsy waiter dropped a tray of drinks.

definition: To make someone legally or morally bound to an action or course of action; to do someone a favor.
example: The law obliges parents to send their children to school.

definition: A state of great disturbance, confusion, or uncertainty.
example: The country was in political turmoil.

definition: A state of dissatisfaction, disturbance, and agitation among a group of people, typically involving public demonstrations or disorder.
example: Labor unrest led to a series of strikes.

definition: A state of anxiety or nervous excitement; the act of arousing public concern about an issue.
example: She was in a state of agitation waiting for the results.

definition: Surpassing all others; very distinguished in some way.
example: He is the preeminent scholar in his field.

definition: In a natural or raw state; not yet processed or refined; lacking sophistication or subtlety.
example: They built a crude shelter from branches and leaves.

definition: Relating to or denoting an early stage in the evolutionary or historical development of something; (of a person or an animal) not developed or civilized.
example: The tools they found were very primitive.

definition: To leave hurriedly and secretly, typically to escape from custody or avoid arrest.
example: The suspect absconded with the stolen money.

definition: To spread or disperse something, especially information, widely.
example: The organization worked to disseminate information about the new public health initiative.

definition: Easily bent, flexible, or easily influenced.
example: The pliable branches of the willow tree swayed gracefully in the wind.

definition: To take in and understand fully (information or ideas); or to absorb and integrate people or ideas into a wider society or culture.
example: Immigrants often strive to assimilate into their new society while retaining aspects of their original culture.

definition: Curious or inquiring, eager to learn or know things.
example: The inquisitive child asked endless questions about how the universe worked.

definition: Showing an interest in learning or knowing things; questioning.
example: She had an inquiring mind, always wanting to understand the deeper reasons behind events.

definition: A consequence of an action or event, especially a complex or unwelcome one.
example: The new policy had several unforeseen ramifications for the local economy.

definition: To deceive or mislead someone into believing something that is false.
example: He deluded himself into thinking that he could finish the project overnight.

definition: Having ample space; roomy.
example: The new apartment had a very spacious living room, perfect for entertaining guests.

definition: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions; able to recoil or spring back into shape.
example: Despite facing many challenges, the community proved to be incredibly resilient.

definition: To suppose that something is true without having conclusive evidence.
example: Seeing the empty boxes, I could surmise that they had already moved out.

definition: To form a theory or conjecture about a subject without firm evidence; to guess.
example: Historians can only speculate about the true motives behind the ancient king's actions.

definition: Grand and impressive in appearance, size, or style, often in a way that suggests power or importance.
example: The imposing old castle stood majestically on the hilltop, overlooking the town.

definition: Until this time; up to now.
example: The disease was hitherto unknown in this part of the world.

definition: To cause continual trouble or distress to; or a widespread disease or affliction.
example: The persistent mosquitoes plagued our outdoor picnic throughout the evening.

definition: To declare one's public approval or support of; to sign a check on the back to make it payable to another person.
example: Many celebrities were asked to endorse the new product during its launch.

definition: To act aggressively, especially to attack or start a quarrel.
example: The neighboring country was warned not to aggress against its smaller counterpart.

definition: Very skilled or proficient at something.
example: She is adept at solving complex mathematical problems quickly.

definition: Neat, skillful, and quick in one's movements.
example: The magician performed the card trick with such deft movements that no one could follow.

definition: Competent or skilled in doing or using something.
example: He is highly proficient in several programming languages.

definition: To provoke or intensify (strong feelings, especially anger or excitement); to cause a part of the body to become hot, painful, or swollen.
example: The politician's fiery speech seemed to inflame the crowd's emotions.

definition: To encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behavior).
example: The leader was accused of trying to incite rebellion among the populace.

definition: To break out or burst forth suddenly and dramatically.
example: The volcano began to erupt, sending plumes of ash and smoke into the sky.

definition: A trace or remnant of something that is disappearing or no longer exists.
example: The old ruins were the last vestige of a once-great civilization.

definition: To make a mark or design on a surface by pressing or stamping it; a lasting effect or impression.
example: The experience left a lasting imprint on his memory.

definition: Highly respected or admired.
example: She was an esteemed member of the scientific community, known for her groundbreaking research.

definition: To cause (something unpleasant or painful) to be suffered by someone or something.
example: The hurricane inflicted severe damage on the coastal towns.

definition: To drive, push, or cause to move in a particular direction.
example: The powerful engine propelled the boat across the water at high speed.

definition: To drive, force, or urge (someone) to do something.
example: His strong sense of justice impelled him to speak out against the injustice.

definition: Done or made with excessive speed or urgency; rushed.
example: The hasty decision led to several avoidable errors later in the project.

definition: (Of an argument or case) clear, logical, and convincing.
example: The lawyer presented a cogent argument that convinced the jury.

definition: To a great extent or degree; considerably.
example: The new policy has substantially improved employee morale.

definition: (Of two or more facts or figures) differing; inconsistent.
example: The two reports contained discrepant information regarding the project budget.

definition: A small amount of something that remains after the main part has gone or been taken or used.
example: After the chemical reaction, a sticky residue was left in the beaker.

definition: A small remaining quantity of something.
example: The remnants of the ancient civilization were found by archaeologists.

definition: At irregular intervals; not continuously or steadily.
example: It rained intermittently throughout the day, with periods of sunshine.

definition: (Of a place) not seen or visited by many people; sheltered and private.
example: They enjoyed a peaceful vacation in a secluded cabin in the mountains.

definition: Unusually or disproportionately large; excessive.
example: He spent an inordinate amount of time playing video games.

definition: (Of a price or amount charged) unreasonably high.
example: The hotel charged an exorbitant price for a small bottle of water.

definition: Unwarranted or inappropriate because excessive or disproportionate.
example: He was accused of exerting undue influence over his elderly parents.

definition: A tangible or visible form of an idea, quality, or feeling.
example: She was the embodiment of grace and elegance.

definition: An opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete information.
example: The detective's theory was pure conjecture, lacking concrete evidence.

definition: Suppose that something is true without having strong evidence to confirm it.
example: I surmised that he was tired from his long journey.

definition: Capturing one's complete attention; fascinating.
example: The novel was an enthralling tale of adventure and mystery.

definition: A group of stars forming a recognizable pattern that is traditionally named after its apparent form or identified with a mythological figure.
example: The Big Dipper is a well-known constellation in the night sky.

definition: (Especially of sediments) not coherent; loose.
example: The unconsolidated soil was easily eroded by the heavy rain.

definition: (Of spoken or written language) expressed in an incomprehensible or confusing way; illogical.
example: After the accident, he was incoherent and couldn't explain what happened.

definition: (Of a debt, task, or issue) not yet paid, done, or resolved.
example: He still has several outstanding tasks to complete before the deadline.

definition: Not yet resolved or arranged; or, subject to change or disturbance.
example: The legal dispute remains unsettled after months of negotiations.

definition: Not alike; different.
example: The twins look identical, but their personalities are completely dissimilar.

definition: Many and various.
example: The benefits of exercise are manifold, affecting both physical and mental health.

definition: Strange and frightening.
example: The old abandoned house had an eerie silence about it.

definition: Very weak or slight.
example: The connection between the two events was tenuous at best.

definition: Lacking strength and solidity; not solid or real.
example: The evidence presented was insubstantial and couldn't prove his guilt.

definition: Root out and destroy completely.
example: Efforts are being made to extirpate invasive species from the ecosystem.

definition: To present (a complaint, claim, or charge) formally to the proper authorities.
example: He decided to lodge a formal complaint against the airline.

definition: To gather momentum or speed; to become more active or effective.
example: The project began to gain impetus after the new team leader took charge.

definition: Capable of working successfully; feasible.
example: The company needs to find a viable solution to its financial problems.

definition: Free from bacteria or other living microorganisms; totally clean.
example: The surgical instruments must be kept absolutely sterile to prevent infection.

definition: Leave (a place that one previously occupied).
example: Guests are requested to vacate their rooms by 11 AM.

definition: Remove (someone) from a place of danger to a safer place; or, leave (a place) because of danger.
example: The police ordered residents to evacuate the building immediately.

definition: Not valid or legally binding.
example: The contract was declared void due to a technicality.

definition: Able to be attributed to; caused by.
example: His success is largely ascribable to his hard work and dedication.

definition: Make (a bill or other proposal) law.
example: Congress voted to enact a new immigration law.

definition: Arranged in layers or strata.
example: The rock formation showed clearly stratified layers of different colors.

definition: Characterized by conflict, disorder, or confusion; not controlled or calm.
example: The 1960s were a turbulent period in American history.

definition: Making a loud, confused noise; or, excited, confused, or disorderly.
example: There was a tumultuous applause when the band came on stage.

definition: Feeling or appearing troubled or nervous.
example: She became visibly agitated when discussing the unexpected change in plans.

definition: An act of assessing something or someone.
example: The bank conducted an appraisal of the property before granting the loan.

definition: Wrong; incorrect.
example: The widespread belief that carrots improve night vision is erroneous.

definition: Prejudiced in favor of a particular cause.
example: The newspaper was criticized for its partisan reporting, clearly favoring one political party.

definition: A person who advocates a theory, proposal, or project.
example: She is a strong proponent of renewable energy sources.

definition: To make a sound with one's voice.
example: He did not utter a single word during the entire meeting.

definition: Give (someone) pleasure or satisfaction.
example: It was gratifying to see all her hard work finally pay off.

definition: A person who supports or defends something, such as a principle or a cause.
example: She was a strong upholder of human rights throughout her career.

definition: To exist in large numbers or amounts.
example: The oceans abound with marine life.

definition: To be full of things; to swarm or be prolific.
example: The pond teemed with tadpoles in the spring.

definition: Inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large, powerful, intense, or capable.
example: The new competitor presented a formidable challenge to the established company.

definition: To break up or cause to break up and cease to function.
example: The band decided to disband after their final tour.

definition: To order or allow to leave; to send away; to decide not to consider something seriously.
example: The judge decided to dismiss the case due to lack of evidence.

definition: Stated without proof.
example: The alleged thief was caught on camera fleeing the scene.

definition: Of high reputation; outstanding.
example: An eminent professor delivered the keynote speech.

definition: Able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions or activities.
example: The Swiss Army knife is a versatile tool for many situations.

definition: Unreasonably high, excessive.
example: The hotel charged an exorbitant price for a bottle of water.

definition: Present from birth.
example: He suffered from a congenital heart defect.

definition: To encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behavior).
example: The speaker was accused of inciting violence among the crowd.

definition: Not allowing fluid to pass through.
example: The raincoat was made of impermeable fabric, keeping him dry.

definition: Not allowing fluid to pass through; unable to be affected by.
example: He seemed impervious to criticism.

definition: To prevent or obstruct (an anticipated event or action) by taking action ahead of time.
example: The company took steps to forestall any potential lawsuits.

definition: To turn away (one's eyes or thoughts); to prevent (an undesirable occurrence) from happening.
example: She averted her gaze from the gruesome scene, then tried to avert a crisis.

definition: To succeed in persuading or influencing someone to do something; to bring about or cause.
example: The doctor tried to induce labor.

definition: Happening afterward or as a result.
example: The accident and the ensuing traffic jam caused major delays.

definition: To an extent that is large enough to be noticed or measured; considerably.
example: The temperature has risen appreciably since yesterday.

definition: To persuade an unwilling person to do something by using force or threats.
example: He was coerced into signing the confession.

definition: To compel or force (someone) toward a particular course of action; to restrict or limit.
example: Budget cuts will constrain the company's ability to expand.

definition: A person who resides or is present in a place, house, or vehicle as its tenant or owner.
example: The previous occupant left the apartment in a mess.

definition: Understood or implied without being stated openly.
example: Their agreement was tacit, needing no formal words.

definition: Implied though not plainly expressed; absolute or without question.
example: Her nod gave me implicit permission to proceed.

definition: To come together to form one mass or whole.
example: The small groups coalesced into a larger movement.

definition: To break or burst suddenly.
example: The pipe ruptured, causing a flood in the basement.

definition: An act of breaking or failing to observe a law, agreement, or code of conduct; a gap in a wall or barrier.
example: He was sued for breach of contract.

definition: To make an emotional appeal; to present and argue for (a position), especially in court.
example: She pleaded with him to stay.

definition: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; very concerned about cleanliness.
example: He was fastidious about his appearance and always dressed impeccably.

definition: A reduction in the size, extent, or importance of something.
example: There was a noticeable diminution in the value of the property.

definition: The force or energy with which a body moves; a driving force or motivation.
example: The award gave her the impetus to continue her research.

definition: Not dangerous or frightened of people; to make something less wild or difficult to control.
example: The lions in the circus were surprisingly tame.

definition: To admit (someone) to a position or organization formally or with ceremony.
example: She was inducted into the Hall of Fame last night.

definition: To begin or introduce (a system, policy, or period); to formally admit (someone) to public office with a ceremony.
example: The new president was inaugurated last week.

definition: Tender, juicy, and tasty; having thick fleshy leaves or stems that store water.
example: The roast chicken was succulent and flavorful.

definition: Consisting of or resembling soft, wet, amorphous matter; containing a lot of pulp.
example: He preferred orange juice with extra pulpy bits.

definition: Of similar character; related by blood.
example: Her situation was akin to my own a few years ago.

definition: A period in history or a person's life, typically one marked by notable events or particular characteristics.
example: The invention of the internet marked a new epoch in communication.

definition: Unwilling or reluctant; lacking a desire to do something.
example: She was disinclined to discuss the matter further after the argument.

definition: Reluctant; unwilling.
example: He was loath to admit his mistake, even when faced with clear evidence.

definition: An opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete information.
example: The detective's theory was pure conjecture, as there was no hard evidence to support it.

definition: An uncertain belief or assumption.
example: His plan was based on the supposition that the weather would remain clear.

definition: Prove (a statement or theory) to be wrong or false; disprove.
example: The lawyer presented new evidence to refute the accusations against her client.

definition: Make a violent attack on; criticize strongly.
example: The critics continued to assail the director's latest film, calling it a complete failure.

definition: Loyal, reliable, and hardworking; strongly built.
example: He was a stalwart supporter of the team, attending every game for decades.

definition: Done or said without being asked or suggested.
example: She offered an unprompted apology for her behavior, which surprised everyone.

definition: Performed or occurring as a result of a sudden inner impulse or inclination and without premeditation or external stimulus.
example: The crowd burst into spontaneous applause at the end of the concert.

definition: Extremely or unusually small.
example: Despite her diminutive stature, she possessed a remarkably powerful voice.

definition: An instruction, command, or duty given to a person or group of people; an amount paid to an agent in a commercial transaction.
example: The artist received a commission to paint a portrait for the mayor.

definition: The application of physical or mental effort; the use of power or influence.
example: Climbing the mountain required significant physical exertion, leaving him exhausted.

definition: An event, action, or object that clearly shows or embodies something, especially a theory or an abstract idea.
example: His anger was a clear manifestation of his frustration with the situation.

definition: To search widely or frantically for something; to look for a solution or idea.
example: They had to cast about for a new strategy when their original plan failed.

definition: Required by law or a rule; obligatory.
example: Wearing seatbelts in cars is compulsory in most countries.

definition: Providing supplementary or additional help and support; a person or thing providing such help.
example: The ship had an auxiliary engine for emergencies, in case the main one failed.

definition: Lower in rank or position; a person under the authority or control of another.
example: He resented taking orders from his subordinate, who was much younger than him.

definition: A company controlled by a holding company; providing support or supplementary information.
example: The large corporation acquired several smaller subsidiary companies to expand its market share.

definition: Not certain or fixed; provisional; done without confidence; hesitant.
example: They made a tentative agreement to meet again next week, but nothing was finalized.

definition: Be a sign or indication of; stand as a name or symbol for.
example: A red light on the dashboard will denote that the engine is overheating.

definition: Refuse to accept or be associated with; deny the truth or validity of.
example: He was quick to repudiate the accusations and declare his innocence.

definition: Refuse to acknowledge; deny.
example: The company decided to disclaim any responsibility for damages caused by the faulty product.

definition: Reproduce (plants, animals, etc.); spread and promote (an idea, theory, etc.) widely.
example: The organization works to propagate its message of peace and understanding around the world.

definition: To associate with (someone), especially someone disapproved of by others.
example: The prince was advised not to consort with commoners, as it could damage his reputation.

definition: Continuing without interruption; unending or constantly repeated.
example: The incessant rain made it impossible to enjoy their outdoor picnic.

definition: Deceit used in order to achieve one's goal.
example: He used a clever subterfuge to get past the security guards unnoticed.

definition: Heated disagreement; an assertion, especially one maintained in argument.
example: The main point of contention between the two parties was the budget allocation.

definition: The quality of being hesitant, uncertain, or provisional.
example: There was a noticeable tentativeness in her voice when she suggested the new plan.

definition: A reversal or arrest in progress; a problem that delays or prevents progress.
example: The project faced a major setback when funding was unexpectedly cut.

definition: A contrary or opposite state, direction, or position; a setback or defeat.
example: After a series of economic reverses, the company struggled to stay afloat.

definition: Enthusiastic or passionate.
example: He was an ardent admirer of classical music, attending every symphony performance.

definition: Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective.
example: The zealous activists campaigned tirelessly for environmental protection.

definition: Special words or expressions used by a particular profession or group that are difficult for others to understand.
example: The scientist's presentation was filled with so much technical jargon that many in the audience struggled to follow.

definition: Almost entirely; nearly; for all practical purposes.
example: The new software has virtually eliminated the need for manual data entry.

definition: To arouse the curiosity or interest of; to fascinate.
example: The mysterious package on her doorstep greatly intrigued her.

definition: (Of an argument or statement) not seeming reasonable or probable; failing to convince.
example: His excuse for being late was so implausible that no one believed him.

definition: Not likely to be true or to happen.
example: It's improbable that we will finish all the work before the deadline.

definition: Existing, happening, or done at the same time.
example: The artist held two concurrent exhibitions in different cities.

definition: To designate (something, typically funds or resources) for a particular purpose.
example: The government decided to earmark a significant amount of money for education.

definition: Diverse in character or content; consisting of elements that are not of the same kind or nature.
example: The classroom was filled with a heterogeneous group of students from various backgrounds.

definition: Consisting of various kinds; diverse.
example: The candy box contained an assorted mix of chocolates and hard candies.

definition: (Of a person, animal, or plant) producing much fruit or foliage or many offspring; (of an artist, author, or composer) producing many works.
example: She is a prolific writer, having published three novels in the past two years.

definition: (Of land or soil) producing or capable of producing abundant vegetation or crops; (of a person, animal, or plant) able to produce offspring or fruit.
example: The fertile soil in the valley allowed for bountiful harvests.

definition: Examining, including, or considering all elements or aspects; fully comprehensive.
example: The researcher conducted an exhaustive study of the subject, leaving no stone unturned.

definition: To cut into (a surface) with a sharp tool; to engrave.
example: The sculptor carefully incised the details onto the stone figure.

definition: To cut or carve (a text or design) on a hard surface.
example: They decided to engrave their wedding date on the back of the watch.

definition: (Of an idea or subject) to dominate or engross the mind of (someone) to the exclusion of other thoughts.
example: Thoughts of the upcoming exam completely preoccupied her.

definition: To replace (someone or something) with another, especially by force or intrigue.
example: Digital music has largely supplanted CDs as the preferred format.

definition: To take the place of (a person or thing previously in authority or use); to replace.
example: The new policy will supersede all previous regulations.

definition: To move (something) from its proper or usual position; to take the place of.
example: The earthquake displaced many families from their homes.

definition: Extremely large or great.
example: The company made a colossal profit last quarter, exceeding all expectations.

definition: Having the ugly or frightening appearance of a monster; extremely large.
example: They discovered a monstrous footprint in the mud, suggesting a very large animal.

definition: Likely or liable to be influenced or harmed by a particular thing.
example: Young children are often susceptible to colds and flu.

definition: To issue or spread out from (a source).
example: A strange light seemed to emanate from the old abandoned house.

definition: In a way that is usual or traditional.
example: The villagers customarily gather at the town square for the annual festival.

definition: Occasionally or at irregular intervals.
example: The old car engine runs sporadically, sometimes working, sometimes not.

definition: To put an end to something, especially something bad, by taking strong action.
example: The government launched a campaign to stamp out corruption.

definition: To root out and destroy completely.
example: Efforts are being made to extirpate invasive species from the island.

definition: To demand or specify (a requirement), typically as part of a bargain or agreement.
example: The contract stipulates that payments must be made within 30 days.

definition: To continue in a course of action even in the face of difficulty or with little or no prospect of success.
example: Despite many setbacks, she persevered and eventually achieved her goal.

definition: Capable of being imagined or grasped by the mind; possible.
example: It's conceivable that we could finish the project early if everyone works extra hours.

definition: To keep for a particular purpose or for future use.
example: I need to reserve a table at the restaurant for Saturday night.

definition: Occupation of a position of dominant power or influence.
example: The new political party quickly gained ascendancy in the polls.

definition: The state or condition of being greater in number or amount, or of having more power or influence.
example: The company maintained its predominance in the market despite new competitors.

definition: To cause severe and extensive damage to.
example: The forest fire ravaged thousands of acres of woodland.

definition: To pull or knock down (a building); to utterly defeat (an opponent).
example: The old factory building was demolished to make way for new apartments.

definition: To fill (a space or container) tightly with something.
example: She tried to stuff all her clothes into one small suitcase.

definition: To make (something) clear; to explain.
example: Could you please elucidate the points you made in your presentation?

definition: Standing out so as to be seen easily; important or famous.
example: The Eiffel Tower is a prominent landmark in Paris.

definition: Standing out so as to be clearly visible; attracting notice or attention.
example: His bright red hat made him conspicuous in the crowd.

definition: To fill something up again; restore a stock or supply of.
example: We need to replenish our supplies of food and water after the long trip.

definition: To resume an activity after an interruption; to make something new, fresh, or strong again.
example: They decided to renew their wedding vows after twenty years of marriage.

definition: Originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native.
example: Kangaroos are indigenous to Australia.

definition: Inhabiting or existing in a land from the earliest times or from before the arrival of colonists; native.
example: The museum featured ancient artifacts created by the aboriginal peoples of the region.

definition: To destroy utterly; obliterate.
example: The army's goal was to annihilate the enemy forces.

definition: To destroy completely; to kill all the members of a group of people or animals.
example: Pest control services work to exterminate rodents and insects from homes.

definition: To tell someone something positively to dispel any doubts; to make something certain to happen.
example: I assure you that your secret is safe with me.

definition: To make amends or reparation for a wrong or injury.
example: He tried to atone for his mistakes by apologizing sincerely to everyone he had hurt.

definition: To compensate or make up for a wrongdoing or injury.
example: She made amends for breaking her friend's vase by buying her a new one.

definition: To suggest or assume the existence, fact, or truth of something as a basis for reasoning, discussion, or belief.
example: Scientists postulate the existence of dark matter to explain certain gravitational effects.

definition: To have enough money to pay for something; to be able to spare or give.
example: We can't afford a new car right now.

definition: To provide evidence to support or prove the truth of something.
example: The prosecutor needed more evidence to substantiate her claims.

definition: Spreading widely throughout an area or a group of people.
example: The pervasive smell of garlic filled the entire restaurant.

definition: Widespread in a particular area at a particular time; common.
example: The belief that hard work leads to success is prevalent in many cultures.

definition: (Of hair, fur, or skin) smooth and glossy; (of a machine, car, or other object) elegantly shaped and attractive.
example: The new sports car had a sleek, aerodynamic design.

definition: To call on (a deity or spirit) in prayer, as a witness, or for inspiration; to cite or appeal to (someone or something) as an authority for an action or in support of an argument.
example: The lawyer chose to invoke the Fifth Amendment during the testimony.

definition: To make something appear unexpectedly or seemingly from nowhere as if by magic; to call upon a spirit or ghost to appear, by means of a magic ritual.
example: The magician was able to conjure a rabbit out of an empty hat.

definition: To overcome, quieten, or bring under control (a feeling or person); to bring (a country or people) under control by force.
example: The police managed to subdue the suspect after a brief struggle.

definition: To defeat thoroughly.
example: The knight went on a quest to vanquish the evil dragon.

definition: (Especially of a document or something similar) still in existence; surviving.
example: There are only a few extant copies of the original manuscript.

definition: (Of a person) tending to spend much time seated; somewhat inactive; (of an animal) remaining in one place.
example: His sedentary lifestyle contributed to his weight gain.

definition: Of considerable importance, size, or worth.
example: They made a substantial donation to the charity.

definition: In a way that cannot be changed, reversed, or recovered.
example: Her decision to move abroad irrevocably altered the course of her life.

definition: In a way that cannot be retrieved or recovered; irrevocably.
example: The old photographs were water damaged and thus irretrievably lost.

definition: Having growth or development that has been hindered or stopped.
example: The lack of nutrients in the soil led to the stunted growth of the plants.

definition: Hindered or impeded the movement or progress of.
example: Heavy snow hampered efforts to rescue the stranded climbers.

definition: (Of a metal or other material) able to be hammered or pressed into shape without breaking or cracking; easily influenced.
example: Gold is a very malleable metal.

definition: Intrusion on a person's territory, rights, personal life, etc.; a gradual advance beyond usual or acceptable limits.
example: The constant noise from the new factory was an encroachment on the residents' peace.

definition: The act of intruding; an instance of intruding.
example: They resented the media's intrusion into their private lives.
